W0MAN's CLUB.
STANDS PAT
ON MA1R0N

NO RECESSION OR SPLIT, SAYS
THE PRESIDENT, MRS.


HOUSE.

TAKE ACTION MARCH 9

pages.raw_text:
Ther~ is no factional fight in the
Woman's club over the police ma
tron; there is no effort to "fire"
any one, and neither is there any
recession on the part Of the club in
the matron fight.


These three points are forcibly
brought to the surface in a state
ment issued today by Mrs. A. e.
House, president of the WOman's
club. Mrs. House says there will
be no action on the matron matter
until the social economic depart
ment of the club, which has the
matter in hand, holds it's regular
meeting. Wednesday, March 8. The
statement of Mrs. House follows:


To the Editor: So much has
appeared in the press lately con-
corning the Woman's club that l
am compelled, against my wish, to
ask for space in which to make a
statement of facts.


/. There has been no official
meeting of the club or of its Off
Gals since the meeting of the city
council Wednesday.
